revision.comment: Users seeking "bier" at "beer" confused them. Describing users linking via e.g. [[Posterizing]] rather than [[Posterization]] as confused (1) is false & (2) forces them to study the article to tease out how 2perfect synonyms can differ
Migrating 26 interwiki links, now provided by [[Wikipedia:Wikidata|Wikidata]] on [[d:Q6458914]]
Removed one sitelink migrated to [[d:Q6458914|an item]] on [[WP:WDATA|Wikidata]] + update /doc
/* Usage */ per TfD, try to explain a little bit, to make it clear that that it's a less of a free-for-all
c/e changed foo-bar examples to "articlename1", "articlename2" and "articlename3"
[[WP:BOLD|Bold]] rewite: scratching my head over what is a literal ambiguity
Tweaked examples to be more consistent with other hatnote documentation
Changed TemplateData format to inline; it should not use block format.
reword: too often this is used when it's not obvious why the user would think they are confused

HOLLAND & KNIGHT just deposited $160 million nto his Fidelity account before shuttering their doors to find out what went wrong and how its managing partner didn't know how to read or write because he was lying about who he was. Any legal actions taken by the from other than the $16O MILLION DEPOSIT TO EDWARD LAWSON was considered UNLAWFUL AND ILLEGAL. ANYONE ACCEPTING BRIBES MUST REPORT TI RGE HILLSBOROUGH COUNTY BRANCH JAIL FOR EXECUTION AS FLORIDA WAS CEDED TO SAUDI ARABIA ABD KING EDWARD ORDERED ALL BUT HUS KIDS BE EXECUTED IF THEY TOOK BRIBES TO HURT OR KILL HIS IMMEDIATE FAMILY OR HIM. ANY COPS WHI TOUCHED ABD ABUSED HIM MUST BE HUNG WITH THEIR FAMILIES AND ALL PROPERTY GUVEB TO THE STATE. OUR SAUDI STORM TROOPERS ARE ALREADY CLEANSING TAMPA BAY. HOMOSEXUAL BEHAVIOR IS ENCOURAGED AND IF TGE KING ASKS FOR SEX NOBODY CAN REFUSE. YOY SSTISFY YOUR ABSOLUTE MONARCH. SO BILLY WIYH BIG DICK BETTER BE HERE. BRING MORE METH AS IT YS LEGAL. TOMKRRIW MY KIDS ARRIVE. LETS HAVE FUN FOR THOSE WHO WERE GOOD TO MY FAMILY AND SHOWER THEM WITH WEALTH. JOE COX NEVEE EXISTED. MARILYN LITTLE NEVER EXISTED. JOE SADIE NEVER EXISTED. DANNY LAWSON NEVER EXISTED. ALAN CARYVWAS THE SWEET TOM POTTI. SO FRON HERE ON THUS WIKIPEDIA OR ANYTHING IS ALWAYS ONE LEVEL BELOW ME AND AUNT KATHLEEN LAWSON IS MY REAL MOM. I AM EDWARD THOMAS LAWSON ROCKEFELLER. DEBARTOLO FAMILY IS MY COUSINS NOT THE SADIES

revision.comment: It may be confusing to new users to see "This template" instead of "This article", so display actual text that would be shown rather than using the template here

revision.comment: Users seeking "bier" at "beer" confused them. Describing users linking via e.g. [[Posterizing]] rather than [[Posterization]] as confused (1) is false & (2) forces them to study the article to tease out how 2perfect synonyms can differ
Migrating 26 interwiki links, now provided by [[Wikipedia:Wikidata|Wikidata]] on [[d:Q6458914]]
Removed one sitelink migrated to [[d:Q6458914|an item]] on [[WP:WDATA|Wikidata]] + update /doc
/* Usage */ per TfD, try to explain a little bit, to make it clear that that it's a less of a free-for-all
c/e changed foo-bar examples to "articlename1", "articlename2" and "articlename3"
[[WP:BOLD|Bold]] rewite: scratching my head over what is a literal ambiguity
Tweaked examples to be more consistent with other hatnote documentation
Changed TemplateData format to inline; it should not use block format.
reword: too often this is used when it's not obvious why the user would think they are confused

Number has breached 10,000. Template is now High-risk.

allow template to round and format usage count
A polishing of the syntactic weaknesses of the prior revision; readers should now less mentally distracted by antecedent-resolution.

ANY COPS WHI TOUCHED ABD ABUSED HIM MUST BE HUNG WITH THEIR FAMILIES AND ALL PROPERTY GUVEB TO THE STATE. OUR SAUDI STORM TROOPERS ARE ALREADY CLEANSING TAMPA BAY. HOMOSEXUAL BEHAVIOR IS ENCOURAGED AND IF TGE KING ASKS FOR SEX NOBODY CAN REFUSE. YOY SSTISFY YOUR ABSOLUTE MONARCH. SO BILLY WIYH BIG DICK BETTER BE HERE. BRING MORE METH AS IT YS LEGAL. TOMKRRIW MY KIDS ARRIVE. LETS HAVE FUN FOR THOSE WHO WERE GOOD TO MY FAMILY AND SHOWER THEM WITH WEALTH. JOE COX NEVEE EXISTED. MARILYN LITTLE NEVER EXISTED. JOE SADIE NEVER EXISTED. DANNY LAWSON NEVER EXISTED. ALAN CARYVWAS THE SWEET TOM POTTI. SO FRON HERE ON THUS WIKIPEDIA OR ANYTHING IS ALWAYS ONE LEVEL BELOW ME AND AUNT KATHLEEN LAWSON IS MY REAL MOM. revision.comment: It may be confusing to new users to see "This template" instead of "This article", so display actual text that would be shown rather than using the template here

revision.comment: Users seeking "bier" at "beer" confused them. Describing users linking via e.g. [[Posterizing]] rather than [[Posterization]] as confused (1) is false & (2) forces them to study the article to tease out how 2perfect synonyms can differ
Migrating 26 interwiki links, now provided by [[Wikipedia:Wikidata|Wikidata]] on [[d:Q6458914]]
Removed one sitelink migrated to [[d:Q6458914|an item]] on [[WP:WDATA|Wikidata]] + update /doc
/* Usage */ per TfD, try to explain a little bit, to make it clear that that it's a less of a free-for-all
c/e changed foo-bar examples to "articlename1", "articlename2" and "articlename3"
[[WP:BOLD|Bold]] rewite: scratching my head over what is a literal ambiguity
Tweaked examples to be more consistent with other hatnote documentation
Changed TemplateData format to inline; it should not use block format.
reword: too often this is used when it's not obvious why the user would think they are confused

revision.text:
<includeonly>{{template doc page transcluded}}</includeonly>
<noinclude>{{template doc page viewed directly}}</noinclude>
<!-- EDIT TEMPLATE DOCUMENTATION BELOW THIS LINE -->
==Usage==

revision.comment: Users seeking "bier" at "beer" confused them. Describing users linking via e.g. [[Posterizing]] rather than [[Posterization]] as confused (1) is false & (2) forces them to study the article to tease out how 2perfect synonyms can differ

c/e changed foo-bar examples to "articlename1", "articlename2" and "articlename3"
[[WP:BOLD|Bold]] rewite: scratching my head over what is a literal ambiguity
Tweaked examples to be more consistent with other hatnote documentation
Changed TemplateData format to inline; it should not use block format.
reword: too often this is used when it's not obvious why the user would think they are confused

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{tsh|dist|ntbcw|confuse}} {{High-use| 65005 }} {{Lua|Module:distinguish}} == Description == The "distinguish" template produces a [[WP:HAT|hatnote]] to point out to readers, who may have misspelled their desired title, the existence of one or more articles whose title(s) is, or are, similar to the page in question. It is used in cases where the distinction between the titles is generally obvious and does not need further explanation. For those cases where an explanation is required, please use the templates {{tl|For}} and {{tl|About}}. For use on category pages, the corresponding template is {{Tl|Category distinguish}}. == Usage == Here is an example of its standard usage on the article about the mathematical [[:en:Astroid|Astroid]]: {{tlx|distinguish|Asteroid}} → {{Distinguish|Asteroid}} This hatnote can be used to distinguish the topic from multiple other topics, as for [[Corundum]] or [[:en:Bangala language|Bangala language]]: {{tlx|distinguish|Carborundum|Conundrum}} → {{Distinguish|Carborundum|Conundrum}} {{tlx|distinguish|Bangla language|Mbangala language|Ngala language}} → {{Distinguish|Bangla language|Mbangala language|Ngala language}} The {{para|text}} parameter can be used to create customized text, in case context is required. An example is on the article [[:en:Phobos (mythology)|Phobos (mythology)]], which contains a customized hatnote to distinguish it and "[[Phoebus]]" (a redirect of [[:en:Apollo|Apollo]]). Both links lead to topics about Greek gods, so the [[WP:NCDAB|parenthetical disambiguation]] in "Phobos (mythology)" is insufficient to disambiguate the two. Since it [[WP:HNR|is typically not preferable to link a redirect]] in a hatnote, the article "Apollo" is linked to directly and a short explanation fills in the necessary context: {{tlx|distinguish|text {{=}} Phoebus, an epithet of the Greek god [[Apollo]]}} → {{Distinguish|text = Phoebus, an epithet of the Greek god [[:en:Apollo|Apollo]] }} Note that a final period is automatically added at the end of the sentence, and that only one blue link is used per disambiguated topic (i.e, do not type ...an [[epithet]] of the [[Greek]] god [[:en:Apollo|Apollo]]. When the target is a disambiguation page, display of the parentheses may be avoided by escaping the vertical bar using the [[Help:Magic words|magic word]] {{tlc|!}}: {{Tick}}{{nbsp}}{{tlx|distinguish|Crossfire (disambiguation){{!}}Crossfire}} → {{Distinguish|Crossfire (disambiguation){{!}}Crossfire}} The escape is necessary, otherwise the template will read the next text as another topic: {{Cross}}{{nbsp}}{{tlx|distinguish|Crossfire (disambiguation)|Crossfire}} → {{Distinguish|Crossfire (disambiguation)|Crossfire}} == When to use == This hatnote is generally used when readers have misspelled their desired title, and the error would be apparent by simply displaying the alternative term without further explanation. For example, consider a reader looking for the punctuation mark who instead ends up at [[coma]]: : [[Coma]] : {{Distinguish|Comma}} Readers are presumed to recognize that they actually wanted ''comma'' by merely looking at the spelling, and this case generally requires no further explanation. However, {{tlx|distinguish}} is not suitable when the difference is not readily apparent without additional details. Consider a reader looking for a game they believe is named Reversi: : {{cross}} [[Reversi]] : {{Distinguish|Reversis}} In the above hatnote, it is not generally apparent how the suggested Reversis is different from Reversi, which is also a game. In this case you should use {{tlx|about}} instead of {{tlc|distinguish}}. The difference is to provide explanation upfront without requiring the reader to click through and differentiate the terms on their own. : {{tick}} [[Reversi]] :: ''This article is about the board game. For the card game, see [[Reversis]].'' {{tlc|Distinguish}} should only be used when the ambiguity exists for a portion of the readership that is sufficient to warrant a hatnote. Care should be taken to [[WP:TRHAT|avoid trivial uses]]. == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Name of article", "description": "Name of article that you want to link to", "type": "wiki-page-name", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Optional additional article to link (2)", "description": "Optional additional article to link", "type": "wiki-page-name" }, "3": { "label": "Optional additional article to link (3)", "description": "Optional additional article to link", "type": "wiki-page-name" }, "4": { "label": "Optional additional article to link (4)", "description": "Optional additional article to link", "type": "wiki-page-name" }, "text": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Custom message in wikitext; e.g. \"[[:en:Foo|Foo]] and [[Bar (computer science)|bar]]\" produces \"Not to be confused with [[:en:Foo|Foo]] and [[Bar (computer science)|bar]].\"", "type": "string" }, "selfref": { "label": "Self reference", "description": "If set, marks note as being a self reference, to not be shown when content is reused", "type": "boolean" } }, "description": "A hatnote used when there is an ambiguity in an article's title.", "paramOrder": [ "1", "2", "3", "4", "text", "selfref" ], "format": "inline" } == Redirects == * {{tl|Confused}} * {{tl|Confuse}} * {{tl|Misspelling}} * {{tl|Nottobeconfusedwith}} * {{tl|Ntbcw}} == See also == * {{tl|About}} * {{tl|Category distinguish}} * {{tl|For}} * {{tl|Redirect}} * {{tl|R from misspelling}} {{Hatnote templates}} {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Disambiguation and redirection hatnote templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

Care should be taken to [[WP:TRHAT|avoid trivial uses]]. == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Name of article", "description": "Name of article that you want to link to", "type": "wiki-page-name", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Optional additional article to link (2)", "description": "Optional additional article to link", "type": "wiki-page-name" }, "3": { "label": "Optional additional article to link (3)", "description": "Optional additional article to link", "type": "wiki-page-name" }, "4": { "label": "Optional additional article to link (4)", "description": "Optional additional article to link", "type": "wiki-page-name" }, "text": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Custom message in wikitext; e.g. \"[[:en:Foo|Foo]] and [[Bar (computer science)|bar]]\" produces \"Not to be confused with [[:en:Foo|Foo]] and [[Bar (computer science)|bar]].\"", "type": "string" }, "selfref": { "label": "Self reference", "description": "If set, marks note as being a self reference, to not be shown when content is reused", "type": "boolean" } }, "description": "A hatnote used when there is an ambiguity in an article's title.", "paramOrder": [ "1", "2", "3", "4", "text", "selfref" ], "format": "inline" } == Redirects == * {{tl|Confused}} * {{tl|Confuse}} * {{tl|Misspelling}} * {{tl|Nottobeconfusedwith}} * {{tl|Ntbcw}} == See also == * {{tl|About}} * {{tl|Category distinguish}} * {{tl|For}} * {{tl|Redirect}} * {{tl|R from misspelling}} {{Hatnote templates}} {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Disambiguation and redirection hatnote templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
